title: Nava card
content:
{{Top|leg}}
[[File:Ncard.png|thumb|200px|A nava card]]
'''Nava cards''' or '''''NavCards''''' were media of carrying coordination and astrogation data.

==History==
During the [[Dark Trooper Project|Dark Trooper campaign]] and while [[Kyle Katarn]] and [[Jan Ors]] were looking for the ''[[Arc Hammer]]'', Ors thought that there would be found some Imperial Navigational devices or charts that the [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]] involved in the Dark Trooper project, which would help them find the location of the ''Arc Hammer''.

On his [[Mission to Nar Shaddaa (Dark Trooper campaign)|mission to Nar Shaddaa]], Katarn found an Imperial nava card describing the activities of all smugglers. The card contained navigational coordinates for a [[Navigation computer/Legends|Nava computer]] but it turned out to be encrypted according to an Imperial military protocol and could be read only after being plugged into a decryption key box.
[[File:Decoder.png|thumb|left|A decoder in the ISO building]]
After Katarn's theft, [[Jabba_Desilijic_Tiure/Legends|Jabba the Hutt]] captured the [[agent/Legends|agent]] pair and his thugs confiscated the Card into [[Star Jewel/Legends|his ship]], but Katarn fought his way to reclaiming it. Katarn [[Mission to Coruscant (Kyle Katarn)|ended up]] in the [[Imperial Security Operations]] Building, [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], the only place where he could insert the nava card into a [[Decoder/Legends|decoder]] to reveal data chips used for additional information, which he then wrote in [[Data-tape/Legends|datatapes]].

==Appearances==
*''[[Star Wars: Dark Forces]]'' {{1st}}
*''[[Star Wars: Dark Forces Remaster]]''
